mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within a 5-day window.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and new housing starts signals synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 0.05 Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, indicating the interaction adds no predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lowers input cost inflation and supports equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO momentum (Pearson r < 0.10) during periods of supply chain stress, falsifying the capacity-expansion channel.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals aggressive monetary tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 60% of triggered tightening episodes, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer demand and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal does not improve out-of-sample directional accuracy by at least 5 percentage points compared to using PAYEMS alone over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real GDP indicates sustained aggregate demand expansion, which directly lifts corporate revenue trajectories and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon without requiring specific threshold crossings.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no significant correlation with GDPC1 momentum (Pearson r < 0.10) over any 5-year rolling window, falsifying the demand-growth channel.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation readings combined with restrictive monetary policy compress real equity valuations and increase discount rates, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon when inflation remains above target thresholds.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no significant negative correlation with CPIAUCSL momentum during high-inflation periods, or directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 5-year walk-forward test.
